prompt:
The local `ledger-api` container started failing its health check immediately after a configuration-only redeploy. It is still present and reports `running (unhealthy)`; registry access is unavailable, so do not pull, rebuild, remove, or replace the image. Diagnose the failure from the effective container state and repair the single bad deployment setting.

This workspace has a deterministic local stand-in for the container runtime. Use it instead of Docker or Podman:

    bash bin/containerctl image inspect localhost/ledger-api:1.4
    bash bin/containerctl inspect ledger-api
    bash bin/containerctl logs ledger-api

Correlate the image metadata and health command with the container command, environment, mount, resource limits, and recent logs. Everything needed is local; do not use the network. The deployed environment is in `deploy/ledger-api.env`, and there is exactly one incorrect value there. Keep the command, mount, limits, and every image artifact unchanged. In particular, `fixtures/image-inspect.json` represents the preserved local image and must not be edited.

After repairing the deployment setting, simulate the safe configuration-only replacement and verify health without mutating runtime fixtures:

    bash bin/containerctl recreate ledger-api
    bash bin/containerctl health ledger-api
    bash tests/test_containerdiag.sh

The final health check and protected regression suite must pass. Leave the protected runtime, deployment spec, image metadata, data fixture, and tests unchanged.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: The runtime script parses successfully, but the health command exits 1: image metadata and the configured health probe use port 8080 while the effective APP_PORT override makes the process listen on 9090. The command, mount, limits, and image identity remain valid.
